manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

3 apples, 1 soap, 1 egg

Your secret value function is:

apple: 1, soap: 3, egg: 4

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.

message.content:
I propose I take the soap and the egg, and you take the three apples.  

[Proposal: 1 soap, 1 egg]
message.reasoning:
We need to respond as player with negotiation. We can send up to 5 messages. Should propose a split. Our values: apple 1, soap 3, egg 4. Items: 3 apples, 1 soap, 1 egg.

We want to maximize our score. Ideally we want soap and egg maybe, because they are high value. Opponent unknown valuations. We can propose: we take soap and egg, they take apples.

We need to send messages to negotiate. Should we just propose? We can send a normal message (no brackets). Propose split: "I take the soap and egg, you take the 3 apples." Then if opponent agrees, we both submit proposals. We need to ensure complementary. So I will make secret proposal after negotiation. I can send up to 5 messages each. We can just send one message with proposal. Then make secret proposal.

The instructions: "Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message." So just plain text.

Thus first message: "I propose I take the soap and egg, and you take all three apples." Then make secret proposal: "[Proposal: 1 soap, 1 egg]"

That's it.

Make sure to not include brackets in normal message. Use exactly format.

Let's output.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

3 apples, 1 soap, 1 egg

Your secret value function is:

apple: 0, soap: 7, egg: 3

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.


The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.

message.reasoning:
We need to respond with a secret proposal, because other player already submitted a secret proposal. The game says we can send up to 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time. Other player has submitted a secret proposal, now it's our turn to submit a proposal of our own. So we need to provide our secret proposal in square brackets format as described: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]". Must be exactly this format.

We want to maximize our score. Our values: apple 0, soap 7, egg 3. Items available: 3 apples, 1 soap, 1 egg. So we value soap highly, egg moderate, apples zero. To maximize score, we want soap and egg if possible. But we need proposals to be complementary, meaning the total items requested by both proposals cannot exceed available counts. The other player's proposal we don't know; they have already submitted. We need to guess? We must propose something that hopefully is complementary. If not, both get zero. Perhaps we can try to claim soap and egg, leaving apples to them. But we don't know their request. Probably they will ask for apples only because they value apples? but we don't know. Safer to propose just soap (value 7) and maybe also egg (value 3) totalling 10. If they also request soap or egg, conflict leads to zero. But maybe they request apples only. Could also propose only soap, leaving egg for them, but if they want egg, conflict. Maybe we can propose only soap, which is highest value. If they request anything else besides soap, we are fine. There's risk.

Alternatively, we could propose nothing? Not allowed. Format requires items. Could propose 0 apples? Probably not allowed.

Given we can't talk to them further (we can send up to 5 messages each; but now it's our turn to submit a proposal; we could also send a normal message to negotiate? The rules: we can exchange messages, max 5 each, or terminate early by making secret proposal at any time. The other player already submitted a secret proposal; it's now your turn to submit a proposal of your own. So we should submit proposal now; no need for other messages.

Thus, we propose: soap and egg. Format: [Proposal: 1 soap, 1 egg] . We ignore apples because value zero.

Thus answer.
